ddls:
CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., distance, speed) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the sensor */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  "model_number" TEXT /* Model number of the sensor */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_readings" (
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor reading */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that took the reading */,
  "reading_value" TEXT /* Value of the sensor reading (e.g., close, far) */,
  "reading_time" TEXT /* Time the reading was taken */,
  "ignore" INTEGER /* Flag indicating whether the reading should be ignored */,
  "confidence_level" REAL /* Level of confidence in the reading (e.g., 0-1) */,
  "data_quality" REAL /* Quality of the data (e.g., 0-1) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reading_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_readings_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sides" (
  "side_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each side */,
  "side_name" TEXT /* Name of the side (e.g., left, right, opposite) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the side */,
  "relative_position" TEXT /* Relative position of the side (e.g., front, rear, center) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("side_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "prev_distances" (
  "distance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each previous distance */,
  "side_id" INTEGER /* ID of the side */,
  "distance_value" TEXT /* Value of the previous distance (e.g., close, far) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the previous distance */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the previous distance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("distance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_prev_distances_side_id FOREIGN KEY ("side_id") REFERENCES sides ("side_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "new_distances" (
  "distance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each new distance */,
  "side_id" INTEGER /* ID of the side */,
  "distance_value" TEXT /* Value of the new distance (e.g., close, far) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the new distance */,
  "new_value_arrives" TEXT /* Expected arrival time of the new value (e.g., soon, later) */,
  "expected_time_to_collision" REAL /* Expected time to collision (e.g., seconds) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("distance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_new_distances_side_id FOREIGN KEY ("side_id") REFERENCES sides ("side_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., collision, obstacle) */,
  "alert_level" TEXT /* Level of the alert (e.g., critical, warning) */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alerts_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alert_history" (
  "alert_history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert history entry */,
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* ID of the alert */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of the alert resolution */,
  "resolution" TEXT /* Resolution of the alert (e.g., resolved, ignored) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_alert_history_alert_id FOREIGN KEY ("alert_id") REFERENCES alerts ("alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metadata entry */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor reading */,
  "metadata_key" TEXT /* Key of the metadata (e.g., sensor_gain, sensor_offset) */,
  "metadata_value" TEXT /* Value of the metadata */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_metadata_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES sensor_readings ("reading_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric entry */,
  "reading_id" INTEGER /* ID of the sensor reading */,
  "metric_name" TEXT /* Name of the metric (e.g., mean, std_dev) */,
  "metric_value" REAL /* Value of the metric */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_metrics_reading_id FOREIGN KEY ("reading_id") REFERENCES sensor_readings ("reading_id")
)
